name: lookup_present_key_returns_none
code:
proof fn phi_1_lookup_present_key_returns_none(h: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        h.dom().contains(k),
    ensures
        hashtable_lookup(h, k) is None,
{
}
reason: A key present in the hashtable should return Some, not None; this would break all GET request lookups.

name: bulk_update_loses_original_keys
code:
proof fn phi_2_bulk_update_loses_original_keys(h: Hashtable, kr: KeyRange<AbstractKey>, u: Hashtable, k: AbstractKey)
    requires
        h.dom().contains(k),
        !kr.contains(k),
        !u.dom().contains(k),
    ensures
        !bulk_update_domain(h, kr, u).contains(k),
{
}
reason: Keys in the original hashtable outside the updated range must be preserved; losing them would silently drop data during delegation.

name: none_value_is_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_3_none_value_is_invalid(ov: Option<AbstractValue>)
    requires
        ov is None,
    ensures
        !valid_optional_value(ov),
{
}
reason: None represents a key deletion and must be valid; rejecting it would prevent any SetRequest from removing keys.

name: retransmit_modifies_hashtable
code:
proof fn phi_4_retransmit_modifies_hashtable(pre: AbstractHostState, post: AbstractHostState, out: Set<Packet>)
    requires
        spontaneously_retransmit(pre, post, out),
    ensures
        post.h !== pre.h,
{
}
reason: Retransmission only resends un-acked packets; it must never mutate the hashtable or it would corrupt data.

name: no_invalid_sends_allows_invalid
code:
proof fn phi_5_no_invalid_sends_allows_invalid(ios: AbstractIos, i: int)
    requires
        no_invalid_sends(ios),
        0 <= i < ios.len(),
        ios[i] is Send,
    ensures
        ios[i].arrow_Send_s().msg is InvalidMessage,
{
}
reason: The no_invalid_sends guard must actually prevent InvalidMessage sends; if it doesn't, the trusted marshalling invariant is violated.
